title: Run Gemma on the edge with the Coral Board
summary: Google's Coral Board is a low-power development board with a Coral NPU accelerator designed to run lightweight models like Gemma on edge devices. The board ships with I/O peripherals including screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and supports applications like live translation, voice control, and multimodal inference entirely on-device.
writeup: This video introduces Google's Coral Board, a low-power development board built around a Coral NPU accelerator for running lightweight models such as Gemma directly on edge devices. It matters because it demonstrates full on-device inference without reliance on cloud connectivity. Key takeaways: the board ships with I/O peripherals including a screen, camera, microphones, and LEDs, and it supports applications like live translation, voice control, and multimodal inference run entirely locally.
